title: Jak Bremen
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Otheruses|Jak|more=Bremen}} {{MultipleIssues|expand|citation}} {{Character |type=New Republic |image=[[File:Jak_Bremen.jpg]] |name=Jak Bremen |homeworld=[[Shiffrin]]''[[The Last Command Sourcebook]]'' |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Brown]][[The Last Command 3|''The Last Command'' 3]] |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] *[[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] **[[Palace Security]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Colonel Bremen, I want to talk to you a minute.''"
"''If it's about Mara Jade, Solo, I don't want to hear it.|[[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] Bremen about [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]]|''[[The Last Command]]''}} '''Jak Bremen''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Colonel/Legends|colonel]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] serving as [[Director/Legends|Director]] of [[Palace Security|Security]] for the New Republic [[Inner Council (New Republic)|Inner Council]] during the campaigns of [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] against the New Republic around [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]]. ==Biography== ===Early life=== A native of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Shiffrin]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]], Jak Bremen was the son of [[Kallin Bremen]], a farmer who organized a group of disgruntled landowners in peaceful protest against the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] forced acquisition of land for the mining of [[hfredium]]. Kallin Bremen was accused of being a "[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] agitator" and was subsequently executed on the orders of the sector [[Moff/Legends|Moff]]. Jak Bremen, himself only a teenager, fled from the planet aboard a mining transport and eventually settled on a planet in the Outer Rim called [[Coveway]]. The hard-working ethic of the people of Coveway had a serious impact on Bremen's personality, and he eventually came to be the colony's [[Constable/Legends|constable]], [[Police officer/Legends|enforcing]] the [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|law]], but mostly fending off attacks by dangerous animals. At some point during his time a constable, he married a local woman, and the couple eventually had a daughter. One time, returning from a hunting expedition, Jak Bremen found that his village had been destroyed, and his wife and infant daughter had been murdered. He tracked the killers to a hidden Imperial garrison established by [[Commission for the Preservation of the New Order/Legends|COMPNOR]] in order to complete a mission. There, he discovered that the garrison commander was responsible for the killings, having deemed it efficient to complete their objective. Though angry, Bremen was cautious and knew that he had neither the means nor capability to do a thing. So he fled, eventually winding up in another village. Shortly after his arrival, he met with an operative of the Rebel Alliance, who informed him of a strike team that was planning to destroy the Imperial garrison. Knowing the garrison's defense networks, Bremen offered to help them in exchange for transport off-planet—the team agreed, and the Imperial garrison was successful neutralized. ===Rebel Alliance=== Bremen entered into the Rebel Alliance military and had his first duty station as a security officer aboard the cruiser [[Starveil (cruiser)|''Starveil'']], a warship tasked with "hit-and-fade" operations near [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]]. Due to his previous experience as a security officer, Bremen quickly rose through the ranks, eventually becoming chief of security aboard the ''Starveil''. ===New Republic=== After the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] and the [[Liberation of Coruscant (Galactic Civil War)|Liberation of Coruscant]] in [[6 ABY/Legends|6 ABY]], Bremen was promoted to [[Colonel/Legends|colonel]] and transferred to [[Imperial Palace/Legends|Imperial Palace]] [[Palace Security|Security]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. He quickly rose to Director of Security for the New Republic Council. Though a competent soldier and security officer, Bremen's security was nonetheless breached when Grand Admiral Thrawn sent a strike team of [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] commandos to [[Raid on Coruscant (Thrawn Crisis)|intercept and abduct]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] and her infant [[Solo twins|twins]], [[Darth Caedus|Jacen]] and [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]]. He was not punished for the attack, but he was very embarrassed and immediately took measures to tighten security throughout the Palace. He later decided to arrest [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]] when she was revealed as an [[Emperor's Hand/Legends|Emperor's Hand]] and possibly implicated in the attack. When Coruscant [[Second Battle of Coruscant|again came under attack]], Bremen was placed on the requisitioned courier [[Messenger (starship)|''Messenger'']] to ensure that word of the siege reached the [[Borderlands fleet]]. ==Personality and traits== Though not quite a rude man, Bremen was more comfortable at work than socializing. A strict commander, he was well-known for his "by the book" strategies, something that had put him at odds with less orthodox officials within the New Republic, especially [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[General/Legends|General]] [[Garm Bel Iblis/Legends|Garm Bel Iblis]]. In light of these interactions, he was also not one to be awestruck by popular figures such as the [[Skywalker family/Legends|Skywalker family]] or the [[Solo family/Legends|Solo clan]], once being quoted as saying, "Everyone else has done more than their fair share of fighting. And dying." He was fully dedicated to the New Republic, and nursed a deep and bitter hatred towards the Empire (more than likely caused by the murders of his father, wife, and daughter), which explained his hostility towards former [[Emperor's Hand/Legends|Emperor's Hand]] [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]]. ==Appearances== *''[[The Last Command]]'' {{Ab|The Last Command (unabridged audiobook)}} {{1st|in book}} *[[The Last Command 3|''The Last Command'' 3]] *{{JournalCite|7|Retreat from Coruscant|reprint=1}} ==Sources== *''[[The Last Command Sourcebook]]'' {{1stID|Jak}} *''[[The Thrawn Trilogy Sourcebook]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{StartBox}} {{SuccessionBox |title=[[Imperial Palace/Legends|Imperial Palace]] [[Chief of Security/Legends|Chief of Security]] |years=[[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]]–[[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]] |before= |before-years= |after=Eventually [[Mirith Sinn]] |after-years=[[13 ABY/Legends|13 ABY]]—}} {{EndBox}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Bremen, Jak}} [[Category:Alliance Military officers]] [[Category:Farmers]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:New Republic Defense Force officers]] [[Category:Police]]
